Intraday post (18/Feb)

Weekly post:

OpEx, VIXpery and PCE week | Weekly post (17-20/Feb)

Python quant course is already available on my Substack-site, by an ex-Wall Street quant.

Read it carefully.

ES is holding the support just as the weekly post predicted. Structural pivot is 6880, upside confirmation should be gained at 6905, and downside confirmation at 6860

ES is holding the support just as the weekly post predicted. Structural pivot is 6880, upside confirmation should be gained at 6905, and downside confirmation at 6860

Let’s look at the daily positioning…

INTRADAY POST Coding today’s positioning:

Speed profile is net negative, customers are selling into both direction, betting on rangebound, but skewing towards defending the downside. Upside vvol remains heavily sold.

Centroid is at 6825.40 (I would add 6806.44 too)

Downside pivot is at 6785.39 target is 6759.39The pivot is a “momentum braking” level and the target is sticky and supportive. Based on the local put spread, I would add that market tolerates 6708 as margin of error and a local reversion trade up towards the target.

Upside pivot is at 6884.78target is 6916.59The target is very sticky and pretty magnetic above the pivot. However, the pivot is a real test if buyers are there or not. Only call skew bid can break it.

Pay very close attention to the centroid today as it functionates as a “line in the sand”. It has a vacuuming-effect, but the probability to break through this line is very low, hence it serves as a reversion line. At 2pm, there is Fed minutes today. Weekly post says benign inflationary narrative.

OptionsDepth Heatmap
